title: pilosella auriculoides subsp. asperrima
intro: Pilosella auriculoides subsp. asperrima, also known as Alpine Mouse-ear, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Pilosella auriculoides subsp. asperrima are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have antioxidant, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat conditions such as digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
